KeyValue.value: Layout Growth Test
Verify the layout growth looks correct when adding buttons.\n1. Add 4 buttons to the newly created frame.\n2. Verify that they layout as expected.
Wise:CreateGroup('Debug Growth Bar', 'line'); Wise.frames['Debug Growth Bar']:Show(); Wise:ToggleEditMode()
Wise:DeleteGroup('Debug Growth Bar'); Wise:ToggleEditMode()
Options Panel Initialization

Verify that the settings panel opens correctly.
1. Open Options
2. Navigate to 'Settings'
3. Check that 'Icon Size' is displayed.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); if Wise.SelectTab then Wise:SelectTab('Settings') end
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Cooldowns Decimal Slots Test

Verify that adding a decimal slot to the Cooldowns interface persists across logins.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface.
2. Open actions for Cooldowns and add a slot like 1.5 with any ability.
3. Reload the UI (/reload).
4. Verify that the decimal slot and ability are still present in the Cooldowns interface.

if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings = WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings or {}; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings.customShow = '[always]'; Wise:UpdateGroupDisplay('Cooldowns')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].visibilitySettings.customShow = ''; Wise:UpdateGroupDisplay('Cooldowns')
Hide Native Interface Test

Verify that checking the 'Hide Game Interface' box in the Properties panel hides the native interface.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface in settings.
2. Open Properties for Cooldowns.
3. Check the 'Hide Game Interface' box and verify the default UI element (EssentialCooldownViewer) hides.
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
Auto-Loaded Utilities Persistence Test

Verify that auto-loaded spells in Utilities persist when the native interface is hidden.
1. Enable the Utilities interface.
2. Ensure auto-loaded spells are present.
3. Hide the game interface in Utilities properties.
4. Reload UI.
5. Verify spells are still there.
if not WiseDB.groups['Utilities'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Utilities'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Utilities', 'UtilityCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Utilities'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Utilities'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Utilities', 'UtilityCooldownViewer')
Auto-Loaded Cooldowns Persistence Test

Verify that auto-loaded spells in Cooldowns persist when the native interface is hidden.
1. Enable the Cooldowns interface.
2. Ensure auto-loaded spells are present.
3. Hide the game interface in Cooldowns properties.
4. Reload UI.
5. Verify spells are still there.
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = true; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
if not WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'] then return end; WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns'].hideNativeInterface = false; Wise:UpdateCooldownWiser('Cooldowns', 'EssentialCooldownViewer')
Wiser Interfaces in Action Picker Test

Verify that 'Specs', 'Professions', 'Forms', and 'Menu Bar' appear in the Interface tab of the Action Picker.
1. Open Options.
2. Select or create an Interface.
3. Add an action and open the Action Picker.
4. Search for 'Interface' category.
5. Verify that 'Specs', 'Professions', 'Forms', and 'Menu Bar' are available as selectable options.
Cooldowns: Dynamic Spec Persistence
Switch specs. Verify that Cooldowns from your previous spec do not incorrectly appear in the new spec. Verify the actions are correctly tagged with the new specRequirements.

local group = WiseDB.groups['Cooldowns']; if not group or not group.actions then print('Cooldowns group not initialized yet.') return end; local foundSpecSpells = 0; local foundGlobalSpells = 0; for _, states in pairs(group.actions) do for _, state in ipairs(states) do if state.autoLoaded then if state.category == 'spec' and type(state.specRequirements) == 'table' then foundSpecSpells = foundSpecSpells + 1 elseif state.category == 'global' then foundGlobalSpells = foundGlobalSpells + 1 end end end end; print(string.format('Found %d Spec-Restricted Spells, %d Global Spells.', foundSpecSpells, foundGlobalSpells)); if foundSpecSpells ~= 0 then print('Test Passed: Dynamically loaded spells are properly spec-restricted.') else print('Note: If you are above level 10 and have a spec, you should see spec-restricted spells.') end
Spec and Equipment Changer Test

Verify that the Spec and Equipment Changer Wiser Interface appears in the Options UI.
1. Open Options.
2. In the Sidebar, look under 'Wiser Interfaces'.
3. Click 'Spec and Equipment Changer'.
4. Verify slots for specs, talent loadouts, and equipment sets appear.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Spec and Equipment Changer'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Show/Hide Rules UI Update Test

Verify the layout and text of the Availability Filtering button in Properties.
1. Open Options and select any interface.
2. Select an action in the middle panel.
3. Look at the Properties panel on the right.
4. Verify 'Availability Filtering' is the title and the text above the button no longer overlaps the 'Edit Filters...' button.
5. Click 'Edit Filters...' and verify the labels 'Show' and 'Hide' are used instead of 'Enable'/'Disable'.
Special Action bars: Leave Vehicle Action

Verify that 'Leave Vehicle', 'Extra Action Button 1', 'Zone Ability', 'Override Bar', and 'Possess Bar' are shown in the 'Special Action bars' category.
1. Open Options.
2. Select or create an Interface.
3. Add an action and open the Action Picker.
4. Change Category to 'Special Action bars'.
5. Verify that the actions are in the list.
Bar Copy: Viewer Performance Test

Verify the optimized Bar Copy tool for viewer-based bars (like Cooldowns).
1. Ensure the Cooldowns interface is active and has auto-loaded spells.
2. Open Options -> Sidebar -> Tools -> Bar Copy Tool.
3. Select 'Cooldown Manager Bar' as the source.
4. Select a new target interface.
5. Click 'Copy & Append'.
6. Verify that the spells are correctly copied and appended to the target interface.
7. Check the Lua console for any errors.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Bar Copy Tool'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Addons Configuration Test

Verify that DataBroker plugins and Addon options appear in the Action Picker.
1. Open Options and create a new interface.
2. Add a new action and open the Action Picker.
3. Search or filter for 'Addons' category.
4. Verify that installed DataBroker plugins and Addon slash commands appear in the list.
5. Select one, place it on the bar, and click it out of combat to verify it opens the addon options or triggers the LDB action.
Addons Wiser Interface Test

Verify that the dynamic 'Addons' Wiser Interface is created and populated.
1. Open Options.
2. In the Sidebar, look under 'Wiser Interfaces'.
3. Click 'Addons'.
4. Verify the slots contain installed Addon slash commands and DataBroker plugins.
if not Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise:CreateOptionsFrame() end; Wise.OptionsFrame:Show(); Wise.selectedGroup = 'Addons'; Wise:UpdateOptionsUI()
if Wise.OptionsFrame then Wise.OptionsFrame:Hide() end
Empty Slots Highlight
1. Hover over the middle slot (the empty slot). It should NOT highlight or scale.\n2. Verify the empty slot acts as a spacer.
if Wise.frames['Test_EmptySlots'] then Wise.frames['Test_EmptySlots']:Hide() end; WiseDB.groups['Test_EmptySlots'] = nil
Waterfall Conflict Strategy

Verify the Waterfall conflict strategy stacks matching actions and the engine fires the first one off cooldown.
1. The test creates a slot with 4 spells: Frostbolt (no mod), Counterspell (no mod), Frost Nova (mod:shift), Ice Block (mod:shift).
2. Bind the slot to a key and enable hold-to-cast in WoW interface settings.
3. Hold the key with NO modifier: Frostbolt fires; when it goes on cooldown the held key should fall through to Counterspell.
4. Hold the key WITH SHIFT: Frost Nova fires; on cooldown it falls through to Ice Block.
5. The icon should reflect the topmost off-cooldown action in the active condition group.
if Wise.frames['Test_Waterfall'] then Wise.frames['Test_Waterfall']:Hide() end; WiseDB.groups['Test_Waterfall'] = nil
Hide Action Bar 1 Reload Persistence

Verify that the action buttons stay hidden after a UI reload.
1. Go to Options -> Settings.
2. Ensure 'Hide Action Bar 1' is checked.
3. Reload UI (/reload).
4. Verify that Action Bar 1 buttons do not show up or overlap the interface.
5. Toggle 'Hide Action Bar 1' off and verify they reappear.
Dynamic Group Combat Visibility
Verify that dynamic groups properly show items/spells with [combat] conditions in combat.\n1. Verify that 'POTS' interface shows healthstone/potion in combat even if they were hidden out of combat.
print('Verify that the POTS interface correctly displays combat-restricted items (Healthstone/Potent Healing Potion) when in combat, even though they are hidden/desaturated out of combat.')
Item Count updates (Mana Buns)

Verify that item counts (such as Conjured Mana Buns) update immediately when modified out of combat.
1. Place 'Conjured Mana Bun' (or any stackable item) on an action button.
2. Note the count shown on the button.
3. Add more buns/items to your bags (e.g. by clicking a Mage distribution box or picking them up from inventory/bank).
4. Verify that the count on the button updates immediately to reflect the new total.
Role Filter Class Restriction

Verify that Druid-specific spells and macros (like Starfall, Rejuv, and Moonfire) do not show under the Role filter when logged in on a Priest.
1. Log in on a Priest character.
2. Open Options -> Middle column filter.
3. Click 'Role' filter.
4. Verify that 'Starfall', 'Rejuv', and 'moonfire' are NOT visible in the slots action list.
Role Filter Global Actions

Verify that global macros and spells (like target macro or Single-Button Assistant) show under the Role filter when logged in on a Priest of a matching role.
1. Log in on a Priest (Shadow) character.
2. Open Options -> Middle column filter.
3. Click 'Role' filter.
4. Verify that 'target' macro and 'Single-Button Assistant' are visible in the slots action list.
Secret Charges Taint Guard

Verify that having a charge-based spell (like Mind Blast or Fire Blast) on the bar does not crash the usability updates or glow logic when execution is tainted in combat.
1. Place a charge-based spell (like Mind Blast) on a Wise button.
2. Engage in combat.
3. Make execution tainted (e.g. by toggling/manipulating options or bindings in combat, or triggering a taint source).
4. Verify that the spell button updates its usability and charge text correctly without throwing any 'attempt to compare field currentCharges (a secret number value...)' Lua errors.
Action Picker Highlight Spells

Verify that spells not used in active rotation have a red dot, and those in macros have a yellow dot.
1. Open Options -> select an interface -> select an action -> click Change Action.
2. In the Spell picker, verify that spells not bound to any interface show a small red dot to the left of their icon.
3. Bind a macro containing a spell name (e.g. '/cast Dash') in any active interface.
4. Open the spell picker again and verify that Dash now has a yellow dot to the left of its icon.
Spellbook Drag Drop Highlight

Verify that dragging a spell from the spellbook correctly highlights drop targets and highlights do not disappear during drag.
1. Pick up any spell from the spellbook.
2. Verify that eligible slot buttons glow (highlight).
3. Move the cursor around, hover over action buttons, and verify the highlight stays visible.
4. Drop the spell onto a button and verify the slot updates.
Switch Flight Style Known Status

Verify that the Switch Flight Style spell (460002) is detected as known by the Wise action bar logic.\n1. Click Run.\n2. Verify in the chat/output if it prints 'Switch Flight Style (460002) known: true'.
print('Switch Flight Style (460002) known:', Wise:IsActionKnown('spell', 460002))
Spec and Equipment Class Filter

Verify that Spec and Equipment changer slots are class-restricted.\n1. Run the test.\n2. Verify the output displays that the group is dynamic, and that other classes' slots are successfully filtered out.

local group = WiseDB.groups['Spec and Equipment Changer']; if not group then print('Group not found') return end; print('Group dynamic:', group.dynamic); local _, playerClass = UnitClass('player'); print('Current Player Class:', playerClass); local simulatedSlot = { name = 'Simulated Druid Slot', class = 'DRUID', specIndex = 1 }; local enables = {}; if simulatedSlot.class then table.insert(enables, 'class:' .. simulatedSlot.class) end; local simulatedAction = { type = 'misc', value = 'spec_equip_sim', name = simulatedSlot.name, category = 'class', visibilityEnable = enables }; local isAllowed = Wise:IsActionAllowed(simulatedAction); print('Simulated Druid Slot Allowed on ' .. playerClass .. ':', isAllowed); if playerClass == 'DRUID' and isAllowed then print('Test Part 2 Passed: Allowed on matching class') elseif playerClass ~= 'DRUID' and not isAllowed then print('Test Part 2 Passed: Correctly blocked on non-matching class') else print('Test Part 2 FAILED!') end
Addon Compartment Integration Test

Verify that addons registered only to the Addon Compartment (and without slash commands/LDB) appear in the Addons interface.
1. Open Options and select or create an interface.
2. Add an action and check the Addons category.
3. Verify that addons registered to the compartment (like WildUTools, BugSack, etc.) are listed.
4. Click 'Run' to print the compartment-registered addons list in the chat/output.

if AddonCompartmentFrame and AddonCompartmentFrame.registeredAddons then print('Compartment Addons registered:') for _, e in ipairs(AddonCompartmentFrame.registeredAddons) do print('-', e.text) end else print('AddonCompartmentFrame not available') end
Forms Interface Availability

Verify that the 'Forms' Wiser Interface is only available and shown if the player has forms to change into.\n1. Run the test.\n2. Verify that the output displays whether the Forms group is available on this character/spec, and that it correctly matches the status of GetNumShapeshiftForms().

local numForms = GetNumShapeshiftForms() or 0; local available = Wise:IsGroupAvailable('Forms'); print(string.format('GetNumShapeshiftForms(): %d, Forms available: %s', numForms, tostring(available))); if (numForms > 0 and available) or (numForms == 0 and not available) then print('Test Passed: Forms availability correctly reflects GetNumShapeshiftForms()') else print('Test FAILED: Forms availability does not match GetNumShapeshiftForms()') end
Combat EnableMouse Taint Guard

Verify that popup rings/groups can be shown and hidden in combat without throwing EnableMouse action blocked errors.
1. Bind a popup ring group to a keybind.
2. Engage in combat (e.g. attack a training dummy).
3. Toggle the popup ring visible and invisible multiple times via keybind.
4. Verify that no ADDON_ACTION_BLOCKED error occurs for EnableMouse.
DynamicRefreshDriver: No Combat/Modifier Spam

Verify the DynamicRefreshDriver does NOT refresh on every modifier press or during combat (Rule 12 #3/#4 regression guard).
1. Have at least one dynamic interface active with graph slots.
2. /wise cpu start
3. Engage a training dummy and play a full rotation for ~30s, tapping shift/ctrl/alt frequently (as in normal play).
4. Leave combat, then /wise cpu.
5. Verify 'elsewhere (tickers/handlers)' cost is low and comparable to idle — NOT inflated by per-keypress or per-GCD refreshes.
6. Out of combat: switch specs and confirm graph slots rebuild correctly (icons/macros track the new spec).
7. Confirm modifier-conditioned slots ([mod:shift]) still update their icon within ~0.3s of pressing shift (served by the _needsPoll ticker, not the event driver).

print('DynamicRefreshDriver guard: MODIFIER_STATE_CHANGED must NOT be registered on the dynamic event frame; spec/spell rebuilds must be combat-gated and coalesced. Use /wise cpu start ... /wise cpu to measure handler cost across a combat sample.')
Proc Glow: No Repeated Pop

Verify a suggested/proc glow shows ONCE and holds steady instead of re-popping every aura tick (e.g. Shadow Word: Death / Madness on a Shadow Priest).
1. Put the procced spell (Shadow Word: Death) on a Wise slot with glows enabled.
2. Trigger the proc (Madness) in combat on a dummy.
3. Verify the glow appears once and stays steady — it must NOT visibly re-animate/pop on every UNIT_AURA tick.
4. When the proc ends, the glow fades out once and does not reappear.
5. /wise cpu start during sustained combat, then /wise cpu — confirm UpdateAllUsability handler cost stays low (UNIT_AURA bursts are coalesced to one pass per frame).
6. Drag a spell onto another slot and confirm legitimate glows are restored afterward (drag does not leave the glow-state cache stuck).

print('Proc glow guard: ShowOverlayGlow/HideOverlayGlow must only fire on a btn._wiseGlowOn transition, and UpdateAllUsability must be coalesced via scheduleUsabilityUpdate. Watch a charge-based proc (SW:Death/Madness) for steady, non-repeating glow.')
Override Bar Dynamic Icons

Verify that the Override Bar buttons (slots 133-144) and the 'overridebar' / 'possessbar' special actions display the correct icons and update dynamically when entering/exiting an override bar, vehicle, or possess bar.
1. Create a Wise interface bar and configure its buttons to use the 'Override Bar' category buttons or the 'Override Bar' special action.
2. Enter the Torch Tossing event in Stormwind, the G-99 Landshark vehicle, or any other vehicle/override.
3. Verify that the icons update to show the vehicle/override spells (e.g. Throw Torch) rather than staying blank/hidden or showing a question mark.
4. Verify that the tooltips are displayed correctly when hovering over the buttons.
5. Exit the override/vehicle, and verify that the icons revert back or hide correctly.

print('Override Bar Dynamic Icons test: ResolveBarActionID must map override (133-144), possess (145-156), and vehicle/dragonriding (121-132) slots to the active special page returned by GetOverrideBarIndex(), GetVehicleBarIndex(), or GetTempShapeshiftBarIndex().')
